# Transcrição: You_become_what_you_consistently_practice

- **Fonte:** You_become_what_you_consistently_practice
- **Duração:** 01:09
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:09]

In the last 36 years of my life, there's been one pattern that has shown up consistently and the pattern is simple. Whatever I do consistently, I tend to be good at. It's so simple I want to punch myself in the face. It's such a simple lesson, however it's helped me so much because when I'm not good at something in my life, I just think to myself, well, villain, have you done this thing consistently in your life? And if the answer is no, that's why you're not good at it. That's it. It's not because you're not capable. It's not because you can't do something. It's just you haven't gotten good enough at it because you haven't been as consistent. This is why I love the quote from Raymond Joseph Teller. Magic is just someone spending more time on something than anyone else would reasonably expect. What's he saying there? It's just about consistency again. So ask yourself, if you're not seeing progress in the areas you want to see progress, are you consistently doing that thing? And I just had an epiphany. I used to think that, oh, Vin, you skipped the gym for the last seven years. I used to thought I was being lazy. But no, no, no, no, no, no, no. I was consistently practicing to become a professional couch potato.
